---
description: "Use when building or revising frontend UI, landing pages, dashboards, forms, navigation, or visual systems. Covers style choice, typography, color, accessibility, and responsive behavior."
name: "UI UX Direction"
---
# UI UX Direction

- Pick a product-appropriate visual direction before changing the interface.
- Use a limited color system with a clear primary, support, and accent role.
- Maintain a strong heading-to-body hierarchy.
- Ensure visible focus states, readable contrast, and touch-friendly targets.
- Check layouts at mobile, tablet, laptop, and large desktop widths.
- Avoid generic visual trends when they do not fit the product.
- Prefer restrained, meaningful motion over decorative animation.
